message:
perf(cli,context): hoist runtime regex compilation to package-level vars

Four places compiled `regexp.MustCompile` inside hot function
bodies, which re-runs the regex compiler every call. Hoist each
to a package-level `var re = regexp.MustCompile(...)` so the
compile happens once at init and is reused thereafter. All four
sites are on code paths that run on every cycle of their
respective loops (swarm status refresh, dashboard model probe,
context summary extraction), so the savings compound over a long
session.

## Call sites

- `internal/cli/models.go`:
  - `parseDarwinVRAMGB` — `darwinVRAMGBRegex` hoisted out of the
    per-call body. The function is invoked each time the dashboard
    refreshes local GPU info on macOS hosts.
- `internal/cli/swarm.go`:
  - `newSwarmSessionStatusRE` — previously a local inside
    `newSwarmStatusCmd`, which meant each `swarm status` command
    invocation recompiled the session-name pattern. Now a
    package-level `swarmSessionRE`.
- `internal/context/summary.go`:
  - `extractFilePaths` — four patterns (`file.ext`, `path/to/file`,
    `internal/...`, `cmd/...`) lifted into a single
    `filePathRegexes []*regexp.Regexp` slice that the function
    iterates. The doc comments for each pattern are preserved on
    the `MustCompile` call.
  - `extractLastTask` — three task-indicator patterns
    (`working on ...`, `task: ...`, `TODO: ...`) lifted into a
    `lastTaskRegexes` slice with the same treatment.

No behavior change — the regex patterns are byte-identical to
the old ones — but the repeated compile work is eliminated from
every hot path that touches these helpers.
